channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Australia approves extradition of former US Marine over alleged training of Chinese military pilots
item.description: Former U.S. Marine Corps pilot Daniel Duggan will be extradited from Australia to the United States over allegations that he illegally trained Chinese aviators.
item.dc:creator: By Keiran Smith, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Many Americans have come to rely on Chinese-made drones. Now lawmakers want to ban them
item.description: The economic and technological rivalry between the U.S. and China has come to the drone market, where Chinese-made flying devices are a dominant player in America.
item.dc:creator: Didi Tang, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Former Israeli spies describe attack using exploding electronic devices against Hezbollah
item.description: Two recently retired senior Israeli intelligence agents are sharing details about a deadly clandestine operation that targeted Hezbollah militants in Lebanon and Syria with exploding pagers and walkie-talkies.
item.dc:creator: Darlene Superville, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A Holy Year is about to start in Rome. Here's what you need to know
item.description: Pope Francis is formally inaugurating the 2025 Holy Year, reviving an ancient church tradition encouraging the faithful to make pilgrimages to Rome, amid new security fears following a Christmas market attack in Germany.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Winfield, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump threatens to try to take back the Panama Canal. Panama's president balks at the suggestion
item.description: Donald Trump is suggesting that his new administration could try to regain control of the Panama Canal.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow And Will Weissert, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Israeli strikes in Gaza kill at least 22 as Vatican envoy visits Christians for pre-Christmas Mass
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli strikes across the Gaza Strip overnight and into Sunday killed at least 22 people, including five children, Palestinian medical officials said, while Gaza's small Christian community celebrated a pre-Christmas Mass.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Rome's iconic Trevi Fountain reopens after renovation work in time for the Jubilee Holy Year
item.description: ROME (AP) — Rome’s iconic Trevi Fountain reopened Sunday after three months of renovations, just in time for the inauguration of the 2025 Jubilee Holy Year that is expected to draw millions of visitors.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What we know about the deadly Christmas market attack in Germany
item.description: MAGDEBURG, Germany (AP) — Four women and a 9-year-old boy were killed and 200 people were injured when a man drove into a Christmas market teeming with holiday shoppers in the German city of Magdeburg — an attack that has left Germans mourning the victims and with a shaken sense of security.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: At least 10 are killed as a small plane crashes into a Brazilian town popular with tourists
item.description: Brazil’s Civil Defense Agency says a small plane has crashed into a Brazilian town that's popular with tourists and killed all 10 passengers and crew on board.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Russia's Putin holds talks with Slovakian PM Fico, in a rare visit to Moscow by an EU leader
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin has hosted Slovakia’s prime minister, Robert Fico, in a rare visit to the Kremlin by an EU leader since Moscow’s all-out invasion of Ukraine in February 2022.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ukrainian drones hit a Russian fuel depot for the second time this month
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukrainian drones struck a major Russian fuel depot for the second time in just over a week on Sunday, according to a senior Russian regional official, as part of a “massive” cross-border attack on fuel and energy facilities that Kyiv says supply Moscow's military.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Two US Navy pilots shot down over Red Sea in apparent 'friendly fire' incident: US military
item.description: Two U.S. Navy pilots were shot down Sunday over the Red Sea in what appeared to be "friendly fire", but were found alive, the U.S. military said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The water was their livelihood. Now Thailand's sea nomads work to preserve a vanishing way of life
item.description: SURIN ISLANDS, Thailand (AP) — When Hook was a child, he started his days by jumping off the boat that his family lived on and into the ocean. By age 3, he could already swim and dive in shallow waters. His home was a kabang, a boat, that his family sailed in Thailand’s southern waters.
item.dc:creator: HUIZHONG WU and JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope to skip outdoor Sunday prayer after catching cold days ahead of Christmas Eve, Day Masses
item.description: Pope Francis is suffering from a cold and will give the Sunday blessing ahead of a busy week that includes Christmas Eve and Day Masses and the start of the Holy Year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump: Panama will lose control of canal if it continues to 'rip off' US
item.description: President-elect Trump took issue with fees charged to use the Panama Canal, a vital shipping route, on Saturday night, claiming that he will demand oversight of the waterway be given back to the U.S. when he returns to office if it's not changed.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, The Hill

item.title: US military conducts successful airstrikes on Houthi rebel forces in Yemen
item.description: The U.S. military on Saturday said it conducted precision airstrikes on a missile storage site and a command-and-control center in Yemen against Houthi rebels.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: More than 30 dead in Brazil bus and truck collision
item.description: Over 30 people were killed after a packed bus collided with a truck in Brazil; the other passengers were transported to a local hospital and remain in critical condition.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Germans mourn the 5 killed and 200 injured in the apparent attack on a Christmas market
item.description: MAGDEBURG, Germany (AP) — Germans on Saturday mourned the victims of an apparent attack in which authorities say a doctor drove into a busy outdoor Christmas market, killing five people, injuring 200 others and shaking the public’s sense of security at what would otherwise be a time of joy.
item.dc:creator: KIRSTEN GRIESHABER, GEIR MOULSON and VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: German Christmas market ramming is the latest attack to use vehicles as deadly weapons
item.description: LONDON (AP) — A car-ramming at a Christmas market in Germany, which police are treating as an attack, is the latest in a grim series of events in which vehicles have been used as deadly weapons.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Projectile from Yemen strikes near Tel Aviv, injuring more than a dozen: officials
item.description: A projectile launched into Israel from Yemen overnight struck Tel Aviv, leading to 16 people suffering mild injuries, according to Israeli officials.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: At least 2 dead and 60 hurt after a car drives into a German Christmas market in a suspected attack
item.description: MAGDEBURG, Germany (AP) — A car plowed into a busy outdoor Christmas market in the eastern German city of Magdeburg on Friday, killing at least two people and injuring at least 60 others in what authorities called a deliberate attack.
item.dc:creator: EBRAHIM NOROOZI, CHRIS STERN and GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Doctors Without Borders points to 'clear signs of ethnic cleansing' by Israel in Gaza
item.description: Doctors Without Borders said Israel is committing “clear signs of ethnic cleansing” in its war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Joanne Haner, The Hill

item.title: GOP seeks ‘maximum pressure’ on Iran with nuclear clock ticking
item.description: Republican lawmakers want a return to "maximum pressure" on Iran in the second Trump administration and are widely open to the idea of strikes on Iranian nuclear sites if it’s the only way to prevent Tehran from wielding nuclear weapons.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Pentagon says the number of US troops in Syria is much higher than previously reported
item.description: The Pentagon said Thursday that there are about 2,000 U.S. Troops in Syria, a number that is more than double the 900 troops reported previously by the DoD for months.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 13 die as an Indian navy speedboat crashes into a passenger ferry off Mumbai
item.description: At least 13 people were killed after an Indian navy speedboat crashed into a ferry that was carrying more than 100 people; the ferry was on its way to a tourist destination off Mumbai.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia says it has detained a suspect in the Moscow bombing that killed a senior general
item.description: Russia's security service said Wednesday it detained a citizen from Uzbekistan in the bombing that killed a senior general as he left his southeastern Moscow apartment — a bold assassination that was claimed by Ukraine's security service. Lt. Gen.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Netanyahu says Israeli troops will occupy a buffer zone inside Syria for the foreseeable future
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Tuesday that Israeli forces will stay in a buffer zone on the Syrian border, seized after the ouster of Syria's President Bashar Assad, until another arrangement is in place “that ensures Israel's security.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation
